title: Backfill paymentIntentId for existing Stripe transactions
body: Existing transactions on dev/staging/prod may have providerTransactionId = cs_... (Stripe session ID) and no paymentIntentId. A one-shot migration should backfill paymentIntentId from Stripe's API using the session ID. The webhook now falls back to providerTransactionId for charge.refunded lookups, so this is not breaking — but full backfill is needed for admin refunds to work via paymentIntentId on legacy transactions.
rationale: The admin refund route now uses paymentIntentId with providerTransactionId fallback, but without backfill, legacy Stripe transactions would still use the cs_... ID when calling stripe.refunds.create, which Stripe rejects.

title: Add paymentIntentId to transaction list columns
body: In src/server/payload/collections/Transactions.ts admin.defaultColumns, add 'paymentIntentId' so admins can see which PaymentIntent ID is associated with each transaction. This helps debugging refund issues.
rationale: Debuggability — without the field visible in the list view, it's hard to correlate transactions with Stripe dashboard PaymentIntents.
